hypothesis.thesis_statement: Paper 'Sequential Cauchy Combination Test for Multiple Testing Problems with Financial Applications' (arXiv:2303.13406) proposes a signal in the 'earnings_events' dimension. Hypothesis: an A-share adaptation predicts the cross-section of forward returns. Test whether the published effect ports to A-shares.
hypothesis.mechanism: Taxonomy dimension: earnings_events (FRONTIER_OPEN). Buildable on: earnings_preannounce(forecast/express); fundamentals(income/balancesheet/cashflow/pit_fundamentals/indicators). Abstract: We introduce a simple tool to control for false discoveries and identify individual signals in scenarios involving many tests, dependent test statistics, and potentially sparse signals. The tool applies the Cauchy combination test recursively on a sequence of expanding subsets of $p$-values and is referred to as the sequential Cauchy combination test. source.title: Sequential Cauchy Combination Test for Multiple Testing Problems with Financial Applications

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: [DRAFT] The paper's effect may not survive A-share microstructure (T+1, limit boards, retail flow) or may be subsumed by existing factors after neutralization.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: [DRAFT] That the paper's construction maps faithfully to Tushare A-share fields without definitional drift.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: [DRAFT] Sealed-OOS rank-ICIR below the committed band, or hard rules failing after realistic costs.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: [DRAFT] Review turnover at the chosen rebalance.

arxiv_evidence_context_only.abstract: We introduce a simple tool to control for false discoveries and identify individual signals in scenarios involving many tests, dependent test statistics, and potentially sparse signals. The tool applies the Cauchy combination test recursively on a sequence of expanding subsets of $p$-values and is referred to as the sequential Cauchy combination test. While the original Cauchy combination test aims to make a global statement about a set of null hypotheses by summing transformed $p$-values, our sequential version determines which $p$-values trigger the rejection of the global null. The sequential test achieves strong familywise error rate control, exhibits less conservatism compared to existi…

todo_before_registering: Read the full paper; extract the EXACT signal construction.
Implement 'arxiv_earnings_events_2303_13406' in factor_library mapping it to A-share Tushare/Qlib fields (PIT-safe Ref(...,1)); confirm fields are registered/approved.
Run the SANDBOX screen + size/industry-neutralized MARGINAL-contribution test vs the catalog (marginal IC × low correlation — the house rule), NOT standalone ICIR.
SET expected_effect = YOUR A-share prediction (point + CI + horizon).
CONFIRM the OOS window is unburned, then factor_lifecycle (draft→candidate) → sealed-OOS.
